differential_diagnoses: Head and neck lymphoma (e.g., Diffuse Large B-cell Lymphoma)
Pseudoaneurysm of the internal carotid artery
next_steps: Perform contrast-enhanced CT angiography or MRA to rule out pseudoaneurysm
Direct laryngoscopy and biopsy of the lesion under anesthesia
Histopathological and immunohistochemical evaluation of tissue
Check serum IgG4 levels and ANCA titers
Positron emission tomography (PET/CT) scan for staging and systemic disease assessment
Consultation with interventional radiology regarding the risk of hemorrhage before tissue sampling
Immediate performance of CT angiography (CTA) of the neck to evaluate the internal carotid artery
Consultation with interventional neuroradiology for emergent management of a potential pseudoaneurysm
Review of original diagnostic imaging by a neuroradiologist with attention to the carotid sheath
Avoidance of any further biopsy or needle aspiration until vascular etiology is ruled out
Preparation for endovascular intervention or surgical control of hemorrhage
